# Git too many active changes error (2025-12-09 20:11Z)

_**User (2025-12-09 20:11Z)**_

What does this message mean? 



The git repository at "/Users/jhigh" has too many active changes, only a subset of Git features will be enabled.

---

_**Agent (model gpt-5.1-codex-max, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Explaining limited feature message**

The system shows many repo changes, limiting features; users should reduce active changes by committing, stashing, or narrowing the workspace.</details></think>

---

That message is telling you the workspace is pointing at a Git repo with a very large number of modified/untracked files, so the IDE is disabling some Git features (e.g., full diff/status views, decorations, blame, search) to avoid performance issues. To restore full Git functionality, shrink the set of active changes: commit or stash what you can, delete large temp/generated files, or scope the workspace to a smaller directory.

---

_**User (2025-12-09 20:11Z)**_

The git repo should be pointing to this project

---

_**Agent (model gpt-5.1-codex-max, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Explaining repo root setup issues**

The user's git repo appears to be set at their home directory instead of the project root, causing too many active changes. I'll explain how to ensure the workspace folder is correctly set to the project root, suggest reopening the folder there, and provide commands to verify the current git root. This will help avoid opening too high-level in Cursor and reduce noise from unrelated changes.</details></think>

---

The warning means Cursor is treating your workspace root as `/Users/jhigh` (your home), which has tons of modified/untracked files, so Git features are throttled. Point Git at the actual project folder instead.

Do this:
- In Cursor, reopen the workspace/folder as `/Users/jhigh/planview-portfolios-mcp` (not the home directory).
- Verify the repo root: `git -C /Users/jhigh/planview-portfolios-mcp rev-parse --show-toplevel` should return `/Users/jhigh/planview-portfolios-mcp`.
- If you see a `.git` directory in `/Users/jhigh`, that’s the one causing noise; either ignore it or move into the project’s `.git` by opening the right folder.
- After reopening to the project root, the warning should go away and full Git features will be available.

---

_**User (2025-12-09 20:11Z)**_

I shouldn't have a .git directory in ~/jhigh right?

---

_**Agent (model gpt-5.1-codex-max, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Advising on checking .git in home**

It's uncommon but possible to have a .git folder in the home directory, which might be accidental; to verify, list all items including hidden ones in the home folder using `ls -a `.</details></think>

---

You generally don’t want a `.git` in `~/jhigh` unless you intentionally made your entire home directory a repo. It’s likely accidental and is what’s triggering the warning.

Check and fix:
- Inspect: `ls -a ~ | grep .git` and `git -C ~ rev-parse --show-toplevel` (if it exists).
- If you don’t need it: delete or rename it: `mv ~/.git ~/.git.backup` (or remove it if you’re sure).
- Then reopen the workspace at `/Users/jhigh/planview-portfolios-mcp` so Git features are scoped to the project.

---
